mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflect expanding aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are non-positive in more than 50% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate above 4.5% increases the equity discount rate and corporate borrowing costs, which compresses valuation multiples and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces non-negative 20-day returns in 55% or more of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of falling retail sales (RSAFS) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals collapsing aggregate demand and household stress, which reduces earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and UNRATE shows no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10) for 60-day forward returns beyond main effects over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) during post-shock recovery periods signals supply-side normalization and operating leverage recovery, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During post-shock recovery periods, month-over-month INDPRO increases fail to coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) captures high-frequency nominal consumer spending surprises that immediately translate into earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following month-over-month RSAFS increases are non-positive in more than 50% of months over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL) and a high federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) create a stagflationary discount-rate shock that raises real required returns and compresses equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with jointly above-median CPIAUCSL and above-median FEDFUNDS do not produce significantly negative 60-day SPX returns versus baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.2 percentage points triggers a household income shock that reduces consumption forecasts and corporate revenue guidance, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns are negative in fewer than 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in housing starts (HOUST) lead construction employment and durable goods orders, signaling a durable cyclical expansion that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through forward earnings growth.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following month-over-month HOUST increases show no statistically significant positive bias versus random walk predictions (binomial test p > 0.10) over any 4-year rolling window.
